Event: Parents tie her thumbs together to stop her from cleaning
Explanation: Harms their daughter’s autonomy and mental health without any shown benefit to themselves.

Event: She chews through the twine
Explanation: She escapes an abusive restraint (benefit) but likely worsens parental hostility and her own physical strain.

Event: Parents pick on and belittle her mental illness
Explanation: They emotionally harm her and aggravate her condition with no indicated gain for themselves.

Event: Sue informs Emma about Will's escapades
Explanation: Sue’s reveal fuels Emma’s anger and possibly Sue’s agenda while upsetting Emma and disrupting others.

Event: Principal Figgins cancels the club after Regionals loss
Explanation: He removes an activity that benefits students; any “administrative” gain is not shown and costs morale.

Event: Terri tells Emma she’ll never have a chance with Will and should marry Ken
Explanation: Terri protects her own relationship prospects with Will while deeply discouraging Emma.

Event: Emma gives and takes on board bad advice
Explanation: Her inconsistent counsel confuses others and undermines her professional effectiveness.

Event: Emma is capable as a counselor when necessary (e.g., talking with Artie)
Explanation: Her effective counseling benefits Artie and affirms her competence and purpose.

Event: Emma makes Will listen to reason
Explanation: She helps Will make better choices while also strengthening their relationship.

Event: Emma has pamphlets for everything
Explanation: Her extreme preparedness helps others navigate issues and reinforces her role and security.

Event: Emma delivers deadpan snark to Sue
Explanation: Her snark gives her emotional release and status while mildly undercutting Sue.

Event: Emma quips “Did someone finally punch you?” to Sue
Explanation: She gains satisfaction and social leverage at Sue’s expense, with no clear constructive outcome.

Event: Emma tells Rachel “When you’re older, that will turn out to be a gift”
Explanation: She reassures Rachel about a trait that may benefit her later, while building rapport.

Event: Emma avoids being touched, remaining a virgin
Explanation: Her avoidance protects her from anxiety yet blocks desired intimacy and connection.

Event: Emma slowly gets better with touch and eventually sleeps with Will
Explanation: She overcomes a major fear, improving her own life and deepening her relationship with Will.

Event: Emma has a panic attack and trashes her office
Explanation: She harms herself and her workspace during the breakdown without helping anyone.

Event: Finn “shut up kisses” Emma during her panic attack
Explanation: His impulsive kiss soothes his feelings/impulses more than hers and risks worsening her distress.

Event: Emma changes her hair/style as dating Carl makes her less obsessive
Explanation: The style change reflects beneficial personal growth and reduced obsessiveness, also easing others’ concern.

Event: Emma sits in her car crying in the rain over her crush on Will
Explanation: She emotionally harms herself while her vulnerability modestly deepens her characterization and potential empathy from others.

Event: Emma tells Ken she will keep her name
Explanation: She asserts her autonomy while honestly setting expectations, which can support a clearer relationship boundary.

Event: Emma marries Carl and changes her name to Pillsbury-Howell
Explanation: The marriage and name change signal commitment, benefiting both partners socially and emotionally.

Event: Emma confesses to Will that she’s a virgin
Explanation: Her honesty fosters intimacy and understanding, aiding both her growth and Will’s ability to respond appropriately.

Event: Emma sanitizes everything around her and takes several decontamination showers
Explanation: Her compulsions reduce her perceived risk but significantly burden her time and well-being.

Event: Emma delivers a “What the Hell, Hero?” to Will over the wedding vs. Sectionals
Explanation: She challenges his selfish priorities, defending her own needs while pushing him toward more responsible behavior.

Event: Emma confronts Will again after Sue tells her about Will and Shelby/April
Explanation: Calling him out on his actions protects herself and others from further hurt and pressures him to improve.
